The graph of the linear equation representing this relationship passes through the point ( 3/8, ­Øæś )

to solve for k, the linear equation can be found and x=3/8 can be inputted into it. First, the slope formula can be used to find the slope. (10-46)/(5-(-4))=-4. Therefore, the slope is -4. This can be used along with a pair of values on the table to solve for the y intercept of the equation. y=mx+b. 10=(-4)*5+b, b=30. Therefore the y intercept is 30, and y=-4x+30. the x value of (3/8) can be plugged into the equation to find the value of k. k=-4(3/8)+30, k=28.5

A table on desmos can be made using the values of the table. Then, a linear regression can be made from the table to find the equation that fits the graph. A second graph of x=3/8 can be made. The y value where the linear regression graph and the graph of x=3/8 intersect is equal to k, as this is equal to the y value of the linear regression graph when x=3/8.
